Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest Defendants Second Application For Good Faith Determination
Off Calendar Jun-29-2016 Text Ruling

Notice Of Joinder And Joinder To Wong Motion To Contest Good Faith Settlement Application Of Defendants Hertz And Spencer.
Off Calendar Jun-29-2016 Text Ruling

Notice Of Joinder In Plaintiffs Motion To Contest Defendants Application For Good Faith Determination
Off Calendar Jun-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN M. FEDER OF ROUDA/FEDER, TEL. NO. 1-415-398-5398 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. JOSEPH KIM, TEL. NO. 1-925-210-1300 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT HERTZ & SPENCER;
ATTY. LIANA W. YUNG OF ERICKSEN ARBUTHONOT, TEL. NO. 1-415-362-7126 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT JJ BUCKLEY LLC;
ATTY. MICHAEL THOMAS FOR DEFENDANT JUSTIN WONG.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JUSTIN WONG'S Joinder In Plaintiffs Motion To Contest Defendants Application For Good Faith Determination - The motion to contest good faith settlement is granted. The good faith evaluation is "made on the basis of information available at the time of settlement." Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499. In this case, Judge Goldsmith already ruled that the 9/14/15 settlement was "out of the ballpark" pursuant to Tech-Bilt and no basis has been presented to the court to require a revisit of that ruling. Judge Goldsmith presumably relied on the evidence that was available to the parties at the time of the settlement. Mr. Spencer/Hertz fails to highlight what evidence was available to the parties at the time of the settlement that now requires a different ruling. Mr. Spencer/Hertz recognizes that post-settlement evidence is irrelevant to the Tech-Bilt analysis, but even if the "new" evidence is relevant, it is not sufficient to require a change to the original ruling. In light of the foregoing, the question of whether there is still a binding, valid settlement agreement is left open. Order signed in open court. Judge: John Stewart; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185
